fields.name: FRES
fields.description: Force a reset of the USB peripheral, exactly like a RESET signaling on the USB

fields.name: PDWN
fields.description: Enter power down mode

fields.name: LPMODE
fields.description: Enter low-power mode

fields.name: FSUSP
fields.description: Enter suspend mode. Clocks and static power dissipation in the analog transceiver are left unaffected

fields.name: RESUME
fields.description: Resume request

fields.name: L1RESUME
fields.description: LPM L1 request request

fields.name: L1REQM
fields.description: L1REQ Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: ESOFM
fields.description: ESOF Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: SOFM
fields.description: SOF Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: RESETM
fields.description: RESET Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: SUSPM
fields.description: SUSP Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: WKUPM
fields.description: WKUP Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: ERRM
fields.description: ERR Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: PMAOVRM
fields.description: PMAOVR Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: CTRM
fields.description: CTR Interrupt enabled, an interrupt request is generated when the corresponding bit in the USB_ISTR register is set

fields.name: EP_ID
fields.description: EP_ID

fields.name: DIR
fields.description: DIR

fields.name: L1REQ
fields.description: LPM command to enter the L1 state is successfully received and acknowledged

fields.name: ESOF
fields.description: an SOF packet is expected but not received

fields.name: SOF
fields.description: beginning of a new USB frame and it is set when a SOF packet arrives through the USB bus

fields.name: RESET
fields.description: peripheral detects an active USB RESET signal at its inputs

fields.name: SUSP
fields.description: no traffic has been received for 3 ms, indicating a suspend mode request from the USB bus

fields.name: WKUP
fields.description: activity is detected that wakes up the USB peripheral

fields.name: ERR
fields.description: One of No ANSwer, Cyclic Redundancy Check, Bit Stuffing or Framing format Violation error occurred

fields.name: PMAOVR
fields.description: microcontroller has not been able to respond in time to an USB memory request

fields.name: CTR
fields.description: endpoint has successfully completed a transaction

fields.name: FN
fields.description: FN

fields.name: LSOF
fields.description: LSOF

fields.name: LCK
fields.description: the frame timer remains in this state until an USB reset or USB suspend event occurs

fields.name: RXDM
fields.description: received data minus upstream port data line

fields.name: RXDP
fields.description: received data plus upstream port data line

fields.name: ADD
fields.description: device address

fields.name: EF
fields.description: USB device enabled

fields.name: BTABLE
fields.description: BTABLE

fields.name: LPMEN
fields.description: enable the LPM support within the USB device

fields.name: LPMACK
fields.description: LPMACK

fields.name: REMWAKE
fields.description: REMWAKE

fields.name: BESL
fields.description: BESL

variants.name: To
variants.description: data transmitted by the USB peripheral to the host PC

variants.name: From
variants.description: data received by the USB peripheral from the host PC

variants.name: Nyet
variants.description: the valid LPM Token will be NYET

variants.name: Ack
variants.description: the valid LPM Token will be ACK

variants.name: Disabled
variants.description: all requests addressed to this endpoint are ignored

variants.name: Stall
variants.description: the endpoint is stalled and all requests result in a STALL handshake

variants.name: Nak
variants.description: the endpoint is naked and all requests result in a NAK handshake

variants.name: Valid
variants.description: this endpoint is enabled, requests are ACKed
